rag_rules: Place implementation-level AGREE annex blocks only in the target implementation scope, not in component type scopes.
Use assign statements in implementation annexes only for outputs or declared auxiliary variables tied to existing features.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by only referencing visible features and subcomponents in AGREE annexes.
Implementation-level contracts must not introduce new declarations or reference hidden internal paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must use the exact syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Use only existing output features as left-hand sides in assign statements within implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not invent or assume identifiers not visible in the current component scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE equation syntax for boolean logic
